what_it_does: A hermetic acceptance test for the runtime activation caller (ActivationCaller in activation_driver.py). It exercises the default-dormant posture, manual clock ticks, held-cursor rollup consolidation, event-only sense firing, mode-detector toggling, and the optional autonomous loop lifecycle, using stubbed recorder entry points and an AST scan to enforce that the driver only orchestrates non-consequential H/I clock drivers.
observations: Tests COMPANY_ACTIVATION_LOOP env gating for arming/disarming the loop.
maybe_start_activation_loop returns None when disarmed and a live daemon thread when armed.
Importing runtime.bridge does not auto-start the loop.
activation_tick fires background only when operator is idle, not when active.
RollupDriver holds a cursor across ticks, consolidates a new wave once, and does not re-consolidate on a later tick.
sense only fires when a real dict sense_event is supplied; non-dict events fail loud.
Mode detector runs every tick and toggles/suggests/no-ops based on MODE_AUTODETECT config.
_loop_iteration drives one real tick directly; the armed loop actually ticks and can be stopped by clearing the env.
AST scan forbids direct calls to dispatch_decision, resolve_surfaced, resolve, approve, fire_activation, and consolidate_rollup.
Uses a global PASS counter and a check() helper for assertions.
Each scenario creates a fresh Suite over a temporary FsStore.
Monkey-patches suite.fire_activation and suite.consolidate_rollup with a recording stub.
standouts: The H3 held-cursor test is a precise regression guard against re-consolidating the same wave.
The AST floor scan is unusual for an acceptance test; it enforces an architectural boundary at the source level.
The suite directly drives _loop_iteration and a real daemon thread with environment toggling.
conventions: Detailed module docstring states the contract and narrative before any code.
check() helper asserts, increments PASS, and prints an ok line.
fresh_suite() factory isolates each scenario with a new temp store and node discovery.
_install_recorder() wires deterministic stubs into the suite.
Feature flags are read from environment variables at call time.
concerns: Relies on a global PASS counter and mutable os.environ state; checks are order-dependent within the file.
The daemon loop test uses real time.sleep and thread join with timeout, which can be flaky under load.
The AST scan is brittle to refactorings that rename functions or add legitimate helper calls.
Monkey-patching suite methods may drift if the Suite interface changes.
notes: A contract-heavy acceptance suite that treats the activation caller as a dormant-by-default, manually-tickable orchestrator with a strict floor.
questions: What are the exact signatures and fields of TickResult and ActivationTickResult?
How does propose_mode interact with set_rhm_config beyond the MODE_AUTODETECT key?
What is the default cadence when COMPANY_ACTIVATION_TICK_S is unset during the armed loop test?
Are background_tick, sense_tick, RollupDriver, and propose_mode defined inside activation_driver.py or imported?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it proves the dormant-by-default caller, manual ticks, held cursor, event-only sense, mode detection, optional loop, and AST-enforced floor.
intention_signals: The file is trying to lock in a safe, inspectable, non-autonomous-by-default activation loop that only fires lightweight H/I drivers on a tick, never directly dispatching or resolving decisions, and to make that contract testable without GPU, server, or real sleep.
novelty: Combining runtime behavioral acceptance with a static AST scan for forbidden verbs and raw entry-point calls, plus the explicit H3 held-cursor regression test.
contribution: A self-contained acceptance test that documents and guards the activation caller's default-dormant posture, manual tick semantics, held-cursor rollup behavior, event-only sense path, mode-detector toggle rules, optional loop lifecycle, and source-level floor boundary.
summary_for_embedding: Hermetic acceptance test for the runtime activation caller covering dormancy, manual ticks, held-cursor rollup, event-only sense, mode detection, optional loop lifecycle, and AST-enforced floor boundaries.
intention_for_embedding: To prove deterministically that the activation caller is dormant by default, ticks only non-consequential H/I drivers, and never crosses the build-dispatch floor.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: Takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition, increments the global PASS counter, and prints an ok line; used as the central assertion primitive for the acceptance script.

symbols.does: Factory that creates an isolated Suite over a temporary store.
symbols.description: Creates a temporary FsStore, discovers nodes via NodeRegistry, and returns a fresh Suite instance so each test scenario starts from a clean substrate.

symbols.does: Recording stub for the activation and rollup entry points.
symbols.description: Implements fire_activation and consolidate_rollup signatures while capturing every call, allowing the test to verify caller orchestration without running the live 4B cast.
Sets up empty fired and rollups lists to store invocations of the stubbed entry points during a test scenario.

symbols.does: Records fire_activation calls and returns a canned result.
symbols.description: Appends the call arguments to fired and returns a deterministic ActivationResult routed to a non-consequential destination; used to observe background and sense driver paths.

symbols.does: Records consolidate_rollup calls and returns a canned rollup result.
symbols.description: Appends the call arguments to rollups and returns a deterministic rollup ActivationResult; used to verify the held cursor advances and consolidates exactly once.

symbols.does: Wires a fresh _Recorder into a suite's entry points.
symbols.description: Attaches a new _Recorder instance to suite.fire_activation and suite.consolidate_rollup and returns the recorder, replacing live behavior with deterministic stubs.

symbols.does: Seeds a controlled old chat event as the last store event.
symbols.description: Appends a chat event with a timestamp age_s seconds in the past, providing a deterministic idle signal for the background idle gate.
